Skip to contentFIRST ALERT | Extreme Heat Warning issued for dangerous heat this weekend
GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KJCT) - We’re heading into what is historically the hottest two weeks of the year, and the heat will deliver.
An Extreme Heat Warning is in effect from noon Saturday until midnight Monday night for the Grand Valley and the De Beque to Silt Corridor of I-70. Temperatures as high as 106 degrees are expected. This includes Grand Junction, Fruita, Palisade, De Beque, Parachute, Rifle and surrounding areas.
A Heat Advisory is in effect from noon Saturday until midnight Monday night for the Lower Gunnison Valley, the Four Corners, and the Dolore River Valley. Temperatures as high as 97-102 are expected in the advisory area. This includes Montrose, Delta, Olathe, Nucla, Paradox, Dove Creek, Norwood, Cortez, Dolores, and nearby areas.
Saturday’s high temperatures are forecast to be near 104 degrees around Grand Junction, 99 degrees around Montrose, 103 degrees around Delta, and 100 degrees around Cortez. Sunday’s high temperatures are forecast to be near 106 degrees around Grand Junction, 100 degrees around Montrose, 104 degrees around Delta, and 99 degrees around Cortez. This will be only the fourth time since 1893 that Grand Junction has been to 106 degrees or hotter. It will be the first time Montrose has been to 100 degrees since 2021 and the first time Cortez has reached 100 degrees since 2023.
Yes, we get intense heat every summer. People also die from this intense heat every summer. The average number of annual fatalities from heat over the past five years is 1,535.
Make sure you’re drinking plenty of water to stay hydrated and eating well so that your body has the energy to manage the heat. Avoid being outside in the heat as much as possible. If you have to be outside, take it easy and stick to the shade as much as possible. Be sure to take frequent cool breaks.
The black topped asphalt can be as hot as 140 degrees and concrete can be as hot as 124 degrees when the air temperature is 95 degrees. Air temperatures will be ten degrees higher. Your pets’ paws can burn in as little as a minute at 140 degrees and in 5 minutes at 120 degrees. Be sure to keep your pets on the grass - specifically on shaded grass - as much as possible to avoid injury to their paws.
An Air Quality Health Advisory continues until 9 AM Friday for Montrose, Ouray, San Miguel, and Dolores counties. The time will likely be extended as the Babylon Fire, the Ferris Fire, and the Gold Mountain Fire continue to produce smoke. The smoke has been more concentrated south of Mesa County. It’s still on-and-off smoke and likely will remain so for at least another couple of days.
Friday will be sunny and hot. We’ll warm from mid-to-upper 60s at 7 AM to upper 80s and lower 90s at noon, then to mid-to-upper 90s at 4 PM. High temperatures in the afternoon will be near 100 degrees around Grand Junction, 96 degrees around Montrose, 100 degrees around Delta, 96 degrees around Cortez, and 102 degrees around Moab. Low temperatures in the morning will be near 66 degrees around Grand Junction, 59 degrees around Montrose, 60 degrees around Delta, 54 degrees around Cortez, and 67 degrees around Moab. A few showers tonight could increase clouds and wind in their vicinity. If that happens near you, you may not reach these low temperatures. Night clouds and wind tend to hold up the temperatures.
